notes.chain_id_source: The validator META CF carries only latest_block_hash, latest_block_height, finalized_hash, finalized_height; it does NOT carry a chain_id key. chain_id = 1 above is sourced from the validator's genesis.json AND is encoded directly inside the V2 transaction body. Bytes [4..12] of the raw payload are 01 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 (little-endian u64). Both sources agree.
notes.hash_methodology: TxStore::put writes bincode::serialize(&signed_tx) as the value, and SignedTransaction::hash() = blake3(bincode::serialize(self)). Therefore tx_hash_recomputed is BLAKE3 over the raw bytes pulled directly from the TRANSACTIONS CF, independent of any local decode round-trip. Matching the CF key means the stored row is intact and the raw bytes in signed_tx_hex are exactly what the writer hashed under.
notes.digest_payload: The model_hash (0xaa..), manifest_root (0x11..), response_hash (0xbb..), and proof_root (0x22..) fields inside the decoded payload are smoke-test placeholder bytes — consistent with a smoke test exercising the submit path with non-semantic digests — and are NOT real model/proof data. Stage 9c.1 asserts structure + byte-roundtrip; it does not treat the digest payload as meaningful content.
notes.hex_prefix_acceptance: Stored here as bare hex. SignedTransaction::from_hex accepts both bare and 0x-prefixed forms; the test exercises both and asserts that re-emission is always bare.
